title: Task 1: Add orchestration metadata schema and validators
description:
Why:
Auto-dispatch needs validated Beads metadata before any runner can safely act.

What:
Add orchestration metadata schema/validators for action, approval, allowed effects, model/thinking policy, worktree/write-set policy, closeout, session policy, and memory policy.

Plan section:
Task 1 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Metadata validation exists, invalid/unsafe implementation metadata blocks dispatch, tests cover valid and invalid examples, and backward compatibility is preserved.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 1
Production-readiness constraint from user: implement each phase as a usable, hardened slice rather than a skeleton. For Task 1, do not close on a bare parser; require stable API, deterministic status precedence, strict bypass rejection, safe explicit defaults, focused negative tests, and clear evidence.
Task 1 evidence: added dependency-free orchestration metadata validator in pi/agent/bin/agnt_lib/orchestration.py and imported it via agnt. TDD RED: tests/test_orchestration.py initially failed with missing validate_orchestration_metadata; hardening RED added Beads metadata adapter tests failing with missing validate_bead_orchestration_metadata. GREEN: tests/test_orchestration.py passed 13 tests; tests/test_orchestration.py tests/test_agnt.py passed 55 tests; full tests/ passed 88 tests. Config checks and bash -n scripts/*.sh passed. Production-ready slice includes stable result schema, deterministic status precedence, direct model override rejection, implement approval/writeSet/closeout/acceptance checks, default recorded session/auto memory policy, and Beads metadata string adapter.
close_reason: Implemented and verified production-quality orchestration metadata schema/validators with TDD and full deterministic tests.

What:

description:

title: Implement agnt lessons client for lesson server
description:
Context: A FastAPI lesson aggregation server now exists at /lesson and /lessons and production URL https://pi-lessons.st5ve.com has been smoke-tested.

Problem: Agents still lack a first-class way to capture local lessons, push them to the server, pull/triage remote lessons, and turn accepted lessons into Beads work in this repo.

Suggested Improvement: Add agnt lessons with capture, inbox, push, pull, and triage/draft-beads flows. Use JSONL with uuid/date/hostname/project/project_dir provenance and configurable AGNT_LESSONS_URL.

Acceptance Criteria:
- agnt lessons capture writes sanitized JSONL to a local runtime inbox.
- agnt lessons push posts JSONL to AGNT_LESSONS_URL/lesson and marks or archives pushed items without losing data.
- agnt lessons pull fetches AGNT_LESSONS_URL/lessons as JSONL with filters.
- agnt lessons triage can list/group candidates and draft Beads creation commands or create Beads only with explicit flag.
- Tests cover local JSONL IO, remote HTTP mocking, provenance defaults, and redaction boundaries.
- Docs connect lessons to SELF-IMPROVEMENT and retrospective workflows.
close_reason: Added agnt lessons capture/inbox/push/pull/triage client with JSONL provenance, redaction, remote server sync, and tests.

title: Implement agnt doctor operational preflight
description:
Context: Agents can become unpredictable when the runtime environment diverges from assumptions.

Problem: Broken dependencies, provider env vars, wrong Node version, missing Beads/Docker/Pi binaries, or model/provider failures are discovered late during agent work.

Suggested Improvement: Add agnt doctor as a deterministic operational preflight with JSON output, strict mode, actionable remediation, and Node LTS/nvm/home-management checks. Integrate it into agnt invoke/work paths as a preflight/warning surface.

Acceptance Criteria:
- agnt doctor reports checks with status, severity, evidence, and suggested actions.
- Checks include core binaries, git root, Python, Pi, Beads, Node LTS/nvm manager detection, provider env vars, configured model targets, and project verification command presence where practical.
- Node remediation respects home management conventions and never edits shell/home files without explicit repair command and confirmation-oriented output.
- Tests cover report schema, node-version scenarios, env-var redaction, and nonzero strict behavior.
- Docs explain when agents should run doctor after repeated tool/env failures.
close_reason: Added agnt doctor operational preflight with Node LTS/provider/config checks, strict JSON reporting, and invoke/work preflight hooks.

title: Spike FastAPI lesson aggregation server
description:
Context: User wants an immediately deployable Docker Compose FastAPI service to aggregate lessons learned from Pi agents.

Problem: Lessons currently have no central intake server; agents need a simple JSONL-compatible API with provenance.

Suggested Improvement: Add a small deployable FastAPI + Postgres lesson server with /lesson and /lessons endpoints, Docker Compose on an external Caddy network, and local tests/fixture support.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Docker Compose defines FastAPI API, Postgres dependency, external Caddy network, and internal DB network.
- API supports POST /lesson for JSON or JSONL upsert by uuid, GET /lesson by uuid, PATCH /lesson by uuid, and GET /lessons as JSONL.
- Lesson schema includes uuid, date, hostname, project/project_dir, summary/status/tags/payload fields.
- Local tests can run without Docker Compose or with an isolated fixture.
- README documents deployment and Caddy proxy assumptions.
close_reason: Added FastAPI + Postgres lesson server with Docker Compose, JSON/JSONL API, test endpoints, README, and pytest coverage.

title: Run production soak and retrospective
description: Use the Beads/run-artifact workflow on real work before declaring the agent orchestration system production-ready.
acceptance_criteria: At least 3-5 real Beads/run-artifact-driven tasks or one real failure/recovery event are completed; a retrospective records operationalization, recovery, idempotency, and artifact handoff findings; new follow-ups are created as Beads before closure.

title: Production-readiness soak and queue-drain recovery
description: Prove the Beads/run-artifact orchestration loop through real usage before treating the system as production-ready.
acceptance_criteria: At least 3-5 real Beads/run-artifact-driven tasks or one real failure/recovery event are completed; soak retrospective is documented; any discovered follow-ups are represented as Beads before closure.

title: Task 11: Documentation, instructions, and eval updates
description:
Why:
Users and future agents need aligned documentation, instructions, command references, and deterministic evals for the new orchestration loop.

What:
Document Beads/Archimedes/observational-memory roles, metadata, runner lifecycle, approvals, worktree policy, self-improvement cadence, command references, and evals/checks.

Plan section:
Task 11 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: README/docs/instructions/command references are aligned; deterministic evals or context checks are updated; project checks pass.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 11
Starting Task 11 under Phase 2 approval on main. Scope is documentation/instruction/eval alignment for the implemented Beads-first orchestration loop; keep AGENTS guidance compact and defer detailed semantics to docs/tool help.
Task 11 evidence: updated README.md, docs/AGNT-SYSTEM.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/ORCHESTRATION-LOOP.md, docs/RUN-ARTIFACTS.md, docs/SELF-IMPROVEMENT.md, pi/agent/bin/README.md, pi/agent/AGENTS.md, and role-context-smoke eval. Docs now describe Beads as durable source of truth; Archimedes/Pi todos as ephemeral projections; observational memory as advisory recall; recorded worker sessions; metadata/run artifact fields; runner lifecycle; Beads-backed approvals; closeout/health checks; one-worktree-per-epic policy; maintenance cadence; lessons harvest; and new command surfaces. AGENTS guidance stayed compact and strengthened durable-state guidance. Updated role-context-smoke to assert the new transient todo guidance appears in implementation-worker context. Verification: full tests/ passed 137; context-health --strict passed; action validate passed; routing-smoke passed; role-context-smoke passed; check-pi-config passed twice; bash -n scripts/*.sh passed; work health and maintenance due smokes emitted valid JSON; git diff --check passed. Peer review: gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini documentation/eval reviews were substantive PASS and metrics annotated.

title: Task 10: Add autonomous self-improvement cadence triggers
description:
Why:
Continuous improvement should be scheduled from durable project signals, not hidden counters.

What:
Compute maintenance due signals from closed beads, commits, failed/blocked runs, human blockers, context-health warnings, stale artifacts, and recorded session volume; add lessons-harvest maintenance modes.

Plan section:
Task 10 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Maintenance due/create-beads dry-run commands work; reviews can auto-run read-only; refactor implementation remains approval-gated; checkpoints are recorded as Beads.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 10
Starting Task 10 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: maintenance triggers must be derived from durable Beads/git/run/health/session signals, dry-run by default for Beads creation, read-only for reviews, and approval-gated for implementation/refactor work.
Task 10 evidence: implemented agnt_lib/maintenance.py with maintenance modes/labels for design-review, architecture-review, simplification, workflow-retro, context-health, and lessons-harvest. maintenance_due_report derives auditable signals from Beads, git commits since last maintenance, run artifacts, work health, context health, human blockers, failed/blocked runs, and recorded sessions; it suppresses modes that already have open maintenance beads. maintenance_create_beads emits dry-run Beads checkpoint specs/commands by default and requires explicit apply for mutation; simplification/refactor specs are implement actions with approved=false so implementation remains approval-gated. lessons_harvest_plan includes closed bead refs, run artifact refs, session/transcript/memory summary refs, and optional observational-memory refs. agnt work maintenance due --json and create-beads --dry-run --json are wired. runner_tick now surfaces/creates due maintenance checkpoints only when idle, respecting pause status and dry-run/live mode. RED: tests/test_maintenance.py failed 5 tests for missing maintenance API, CLI, and runner hook. GREEN: focused maintenance/runner/orchestration tests passed 23; broader focused set passed 85; full tests/ passed 137. Smokes: agnt work maintenance due --json emitted valid JSON; agnt work maintenance create-beads --dry-run --json emitted valid dry-run specs; agnt work health/audit passed; config checks twice passed; bash -n, py_compile, routing-smoke, role-context-smoke, and git diff --check passed. Peer review: initial gemma output was rejected as stub and annotated; rerun gemma plus gpt-4.1-mini reviews were substantive PASS and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified autonomous maintenance due/create-beads triggers, lessons-harvest planning, and idle runner maintenance checkpoint hook.

title: Task 9: Add closeout and rail-guard health checks
description:
Why:
Autonomy needs explicit rail guards before claiming work complete.

What:
Add status/health checks for unclosed approvals, unresolved blockers, missing verification, raw-tool bypass markers, stale sessions, orphaned runs, dirty worktrees, and failed checks.

Plan section:
Task 9 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Health/closeout commands return nonzero or block closure for unsafe states; tests cover representative blockers and happy path.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 9
Starting Task 9 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: health/closeout checks must be deterministic/read-only by default, block unsafe closure, and use Beads/run artifacts as durable source of truth.
Task 9 evidence: implemented agnt_lib/health.py and agnt work health --json with deterministic read-only rail-guard checks for run artifacts, Beads refs, approvals, decisions, follow-ups, stale active sessions, runner stale locks, dirty current checkout and known epic worktrees, raw-tool bypass markers, failed health/closeout checks, orphaned runs, active runs tied to closed beads, and blocked beads without visible blockers. Closeout readiness now blocks succeeded runs missing evidence, failed/pending healthChecks or closeoutChecks, unresolved approvalRefs/decisionRefs, and unresolved followUps. Existing schemaVersion 1 run artifacts remain backward-compatible; legacy completed artifacts warn on missing historical refs instead of failing. scripts/check-pi-config.sh now runs the cheap/read-only work health command. RED: tests/test_health.py failed 6 tests for missing work_health_report, CLI, script wiring, and ref-aware close_readiness. GREEN: tests/test_health.py passed 6; focused tests/test_agnt.py tests/test_orchestration.py tests/test_health.py passed 75; full tests/ passed 132. Smokes: agnt work health --json passed with 0 failures and expected local warnings; agnt work audit --json passed; work finish closeout smoke blocked failed healthChecks with rc 2; config checks twice passed; bash -n, py_compile, routing-smoke, role-context-smoke, and git diff --check passed. Peer review: full-diff gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini reviews were substantive PASS and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified deterministic rail-guard health checks and closeout blockers.

title: Task 8: Implement one-worktree-per-epic policy
description:
Why:
Initial parallelism should isolate by epic while avoiding nested per-task worktree complexity.

What:
Resolve/create/reuse one worktree per epic, record it in run artifacts, block unsafe main-branch or dirty-worktree dispatches, and model overlapping write sets as dependencies.

Plan section:
Task 8 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Worktree resolution is deterministic; runner refuses unsafe branch/dirty states; concurrent overlapping write sets are blocked with Beads dependencies.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 8
Starting Task 8 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: implement deterministic one-worktree-per-epic policy and safety checks, but do not create/remove worktrees without explicit approval.
Task 8 evidence: implemented one-worktree-per-epic policy in agnt_lib/worktree_policy.py. Added deterministic epic_worktree_spec mapping to .worktrees/epic/<epic-id>-<slug> and branch epic/<epic-id>-<slug>, git worktree porcelain parsing, missing-worktree needs-approval status, protected main/master branch blocking, dirty worktree blocking, write-set overlap detection, and worktree snapshots for run artifacts. Runner tick now checks implementation worktree policy before dispatch, blocks missing/dirty/protected worktrees with durable decision blockers, and serializes overlapping write sets in the same epic by planning/adding Beads dependencies. start_work records the worktree snapshot in invocation artifacts. No git worktree add/remove operations were implemented or run. RED: tests/test_worktree_policy.py failed 5 tests for missing helpers and runner integration. GREEN: focused worktree/runner/start-work tests passed 12; full tests/ passed 126. Smokes: git worktree list succeeded, agnt work runner tick --dry-run --json valid, config checks twice passed, bash -n, py_compile, routing-smoke, role-context-smoke, and git diff --check passed. Peer review: full-diff gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini reviews were substantive PASS and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified deterministic one-worktree-per-epic policy and runner safety integration without creating/removing worktrees.

title: Task 7: Implement continuous singleton runner
description:
Why:
Approved ready work should drain continuously while preserving blockers, pause/resume state, singleton safety, sessions, and run evidence.

What:
Add agnt runner commands, lock/state files, ready-work dispatch logic, pause/resume, budget seams, recorded worker sessions, and closeout enforcement.

Plan section:
Task 7 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Runner dry-run/status/tick commands work; only one runner can run per git root; approved work auto-starts; invalid/needs-human work becomes Beads blockers; sessions are recorded by default.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 7
Starting Task 7 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: runner must provide real singleton state, pause/resume, dry-run tick decisions, safe dispatch/blocking behavior, and run/session evidence seams without installing services/hooks.
Task 7 evidence: implemented continuous singleton runner core in agnt_lib/runner.py with .pi/runner state/lock files, atomic lock acquisition via O_EXCL, stale PID detection, pause/resume state, budget placeholder, runner status, bounded tick, and loop with clean lock release. Added agnt work runner status/pause/resume/tick and agnt work loop. Dry-run tick reads bd ready and emits would_start/would_block without mutation; live tick validates metadata, starts dispatchable work with claim=True and recorded session flags, and creates durable Beads decision blockers for invalid/needs-human/blocked metadata. Worker invocation now supports record_session/session_id/session_name, invoke_run_bundle records sessionRef/transcriptRef, and run_work passes runner session options. Gateway runner_status now surfaces real runner state. RED: tests/test_runner.py initially failed for missing runner functions/CLI; added a separate RED for recorded invocation session flags. GREEN: tests/test_runner.py tests/test_ticket_gateway.py passed 13; full tests/ passed 121. Smokes: agnt work runner status --json valid status idle; agnt work runner tick --dry-run --json valid actions; agnt gateway runner_status valid; config checks twice passed; bash -n, py_compile, routing-smoke, role-context-smoke, and git diff --check passed. Peer review: initial artifacts missed untracked files and were marked rejected; full-diff gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini reviews were substantive PASS and metrics annotated. No services, hooks, launch agents, remotes, or pushes were installed/performed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified continuous singleton runner core with status/pause/resume/tick/loop, durable blockers, and recorded-session dispatch seams.

title: Task 6: Add ticket gateway tool and plan-view command
description:
Why:
The main orchestrator needs a constrained control surface with no raw bash, raw Beads, raw subagent, edit, or write access.

What:
Add a ticket_gateway Pi extension/tool with strict enum operations and a /work or /plan-view command, exposing compact Beads/run context and approved recall tools only.

Plan section:
Task 6 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway/tool registers successfully, rejects free-form shell-like payloads, exposes only structured operations, and config checks pass.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 6
Starting Task 6 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: ticket gateway must expose strict structured operations and compact durable Beads/run context, not raw shell/Beads passthrough.
Task 6 evidence: implemented strict ticket gateway core in agnt_lib/gateway.py plus agnt gateway CLI and pi/agent/extensions/ticket-gateway.ts. Gateway supports enum operations list, show, tree, create_draft, request_approval, resolve_blocker, and runner_status. It rejects shell-like/raw command payloads recursively, whitelists fields per operation, emits compact Beads items with metadata validation and active run refs, delegates tree to the existing work-tree core, delegates request_approval/resolve_blocker to the Beads-backed approval core, and reserves a stable not_configured runner_status surface for Task 7. The extension registers ticket_gateway with StringEnum operations and a /work command; it delegates to agnt gateway and does not expose raw bash, raw bd, edit/write, or raw subagent. RED: tests/test_ticket_gateway.py initially failed 8 tests for missing ticket_gateway and missing extension. GREEN: tests/test_ticket_gateway.py passed 8; full tests/ passed 116. Config checks passed twice, bash -n scripts/*.sh passed, py_compile passed, ticket-gateway extension loaded with pi --no-extensions -e ... --list-models, agnt gateway runner_status smoke emitted valid JSON, shell-like payload rejection smoke returned exit 2 with expected error, routing-smoke and role-context-smoke evals passed, git diff --check passed. Peer review: gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini substantive PASS and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified strict ticket gateway core/CLI plus Pi extension and /work command.

title: Task 5: Implement Beads-backed ask and approval flow
description:
Why:
Human decisions must survive UI/session loss and block/resume work through Beads.

What:
Create ticket question/approval tooling or an ask bridge that records context, options, requesting run, answer/rejection/timeout, and blocking relationships in Beads and run results.

Plan section:
Task 5 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Questions and approvals create/update durable Beads records; final decisions are reflected in Beads and run results; cancellation/timeout leaves visible blockers.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 5
Starting Task 5 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: Beads-backed ask/approval flow must create durable, auditable decision records and visible blockers, not a placeholder UI bridge.
Task 5 evidence: implemented Beads-backed ask/decision/approval core in agnt_lib/approvals.py with agnt approvals request/resolve. Requests create Beads decision issues with human/human-gate/beads-backed labels, informed-consent preview metadata (action, scope, consequences, reversibility, closeoutPath), context/options/default/requestingRun, and a blocking dependency via bd dep <decision> --blocks <target>. Requesting run results are updated to needs-human with decisionRefs and approvalRefs for approval gates. Resolution records approved/answered/rejected/cancelled/timed-out outcomes in Beads metadata/notes and run results; approved/answered closes the decision bead, while timeout/cancel/reject leave the blocker visible. Added thin Pi extension bridge pi/agent/extensions/beads-ask-bridge.ts registering ticket_question, ticket_approval, and ticket_decision_resolve; it delegates durable state to agnt approvals and creates the Beads blocker before optional UI confirmation. Added focused tests/test_approvals.py. RED: six approval tests failed for missing core functions and extension. GREEN: tests/test_approvals.py passed 6; tests/test_orchestration.py tests/test_approvals.py passed 19; full tests/ passed 108. Temporary Beads repo smoke created approval, blocker, resolved approved, and verified run refs/status. Extension load via pi --no-extensions -e beads-ask-bridge.ts --list-models passed. Config checks, bash -n, py_compile, git diff --check passed. Deterministic evals routing-smoke and role-context-smoke passed after aligning stale routing-smoke expected selected model with Task 3 policy output. Peer review: gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini substantive PASS; qwen attempts rejected as stubs and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified Beads-backed ask/decision/approval flow with durable Beads blockers, run-result refs, and thin Pi bridge.

notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 4
Starting Task 4 under Phase 2 approval. Production-readiness constraint applies: run artifacts must be a durable, backward-compatible orchestration contract with tests, not placeholder fields.
Task 4 evidence: extended run artifact v1 contract with optional invocation fields ticketMetadata, selectedModel, thinkingLevel, modelSelection, ephemeralTodoSeed, worktree, dispatchPolicy, sessionPolicy, and memoryPolicy. Added result fields sessionRef, transcriptRef, memorySummaryRef, approvalRefs, decisionRefs, healthChecks, and closeoutChecks with update/CLI support and validation. start_work now records ticket snapshot, metadata validation, dispatch policy, session/memory policy, todo seed, worktree, and selected model. Prompt rendering now states session/memory policy and that Archimedes todos are transient while durable outcomes must be recorded in Beads/run evidence. RED: tests failed for missing ticket_metadata, result refs/checks, prompt guidance, and start_work ticket snapshot/policies. GREEN: focused run_bundle/invocation/result tests passed 10 tests; full tests/ passed 102 tests; runs create/update/validate smoke passed; work start smoke wrote ticketMetadata/sessionPolicy/memoryPolicy/selectedModel; config checks, bash -n, py_compile, and git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified production-ready orchestration run artifact fields and prompt/result contract.

title: Task 3: Add model scoring and diversity selection
description:
Why:
Worker model/thinking selection must be auditable and policy-driven rather than orchestrator discretion.

What:
Extend routing to return scored model choices, thinking levels, reasons, rejected candidates, and diversity groups for review/verification fanout.

Plan section:
Task 3 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Routing selection is deterministic and auditable; direct model overrides are rejected unless policy-compatible; routing tests pass.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 3
Starting Task 3 under Phase 2 approval. Production-readiness constraint applies: model scoring/diversity must be a usable policy-driven selector with tests, not a placeholder.
Task 3 evidence: added production model selector in agnt_lib/routing.py with scored candidate contract, diversity groups/fanout, avoidFamilies policy, local/model/task/context/modality hard rejections, metrics-aware adjustments, and backward-compatible route JSON. Integrated policy-selected model/thinking into run artifacts via selectedModel/thinkingLevel/modelSelection, and start_work now records policy-selected models. Direct agnt work run --model is rejected before Beads lookup. RED: tests failed for missing select_model, selected run fields, CLI fanout, start_work selectedModel, and work-run override guard; hardening RED caught CLI --model dry-run bypass. GREEN: tests/test_agnt.py tests/test_route_feedback.py passed 55 tests; full tests/ passed 99 tests; agnt route review cheap local fanout emitted valid JSON with 3 diverse groups; work start smoke wrote selectedModel/thinkingLevel/modelSelection; config checks, bash -n, py_compile, and git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified production-ready model scoring/diversity selection and selected-model run artifact recording.

title: Task 2: Add Beads plan view and dependency tree core
description:
Why:
The orchestrator needs a machine-readable plan/dependency view without raw Beads access.

What:
Add agnt commands that summarize Beads trees, dependencies, metadata validation, active run refs, approvals, and blockers in text and JSON.

Plan section:
Task 2 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Plan/dependency tree command works for epic and non-epic views, emits valid JSON, and includes validation/run/approval/blocker context.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 2
Phase 2 approval recorded on epic pi-6yg. Starting with Task 2 because the ticket gateway depends on the deterministic plan/dependency tree core. Production-readiness constraint applies: implement a usable, tested slice rather than a skeleton.
Task 2 evidence: added production work-tree core and CLI command agnt work tree. RED: tests failed for missing build_work_tree, run_refs_by_bead, and work tree command; hardening RED caught approval-labeled implementation tasks being misclassified as approval records. GREEN: focused tests passed 13 tests for work/orchestration/ticket selection; agnt work tree --json and --epic pi-6yg --json emitted valid JSON with zero errors; full tests/ passed 92 tests; config checks, bash -n scripts/*.sh, py_compile, and git diff --check passed. Production-ready slice includes Beads CLI traversal only, no direct .beads parsing, metadata validation per node, dependency/dependent edges, blocker refs, approval refs with strict human/decision classification, active run refs, text and JSON output, and epic/non-epic views.
close_reason: Implemented and verified production-ready Beads plan/dependency tree core and agnt work tree CLI.

title: Update GLM-5.2 routing guidance

close_reason: Fixed GLM-5.2 Olla routing by using the routable glm-5.2 model id, updating task/catalog references, and enabling supported high/xhigh thinking through OpenRouter reasoning controls.
